reasoning: The baseline calculation of 0.083 per 30 days indicates exactly 1 event of 'Explosions/Remote violence' out of the preceding 360 days. This gives a base rate of roughly 8.3% for any 30-day window to contain an event. For the first resolution date (2026-02-22), 23 days of its 30-day window (starting Jan 23) have already passed. Extensive searches found no recent instances of related violence in the DR, leaving the probability resting almost entirely on the ~4.7% chance the single historical event from the past year occurred between Jan 23 and Feb 9, plus the ~2% chance of a new event in the next 7 days. So ~8% is appropriate. The second resolution date (2026-03-17) covers a 30-day window purely in the future (Feb 15 - Mar 17). It relies on the historical base rate (~8.3%), slightly adjusted towards 0.5 for a more distant horizon and accounting for potential minor spillover from Haitian gang activity, making 10% a well-calibrated estimate and maintaining the increasing uncertainty required for later dates.
